title: How to Make $20,000/Month with Claude Code
summary: Liam Ottley argues that retainer-based AI agency models for small and medium-sized businesses are now viable and profitable due to Claude Code, enabling recurring monthly revenue starting at $2,500 and scaling to $20,000+. The model involves deploying AI Operating Systems on-site and providing ongoing management and optimization services.
writeup: The video argues that Claude Code and AI operating systems have dropped development costs far enough that AI agencies can now profitably run the subscription retainer model that was financially unworkable in 2023. The core case study is an accelerator member named Tyler who flies to client sites, installs a Claude Code workspace integrated into the client's tools, delivers the first automation in person to prove value, then switches the relationship to a monthly retainer of roughly $2,500. The through-line is that as build time collapses, agency value shifts from one-off project delivery toward ongoing management, optimization, and training, and those recurring fees compound fastest when an agency niches down to a single industry and reuses solutions across clients.
key_points: The Designjoy retainer model (Brett's one-person design business charging $3,000 to $5,000 per month for a capped queue of design deliverables) was attempted for automations at Morningside AI in 2023 but required $20,000 to $30,000 per month to be profitable because development hours were still too high; Claude Code has since collapsed that cost barrier enough to make $2,500 per month viable.

Tyler's go-to-market sequence: fly out to the client, install the Claude Code AI operating system with all relevant API keys and platform integrations, build the first agreed automation on-site to prove ROI immediately, then return home and manage an ongoing request queue remotely for a flat monthly retainer of $2,500.

The agency compounds value by stacking fees: a base retainer for new automations, then separate monthly management fees (around $300 to $500 per system) and optimization fees for each live system, so total monthly revenue per client grows automatically as more systems are delivered.

Niching to a single industry creates a flywheel: solutions built for one client can be exported and reinstalled for the next with minimal rework, and learnings carry over, making each successive client cheaper and faster to serve.

The two strategic paths for a retainer agency are wide (many clients, small retainers) and deep (few clients, stacking fees on a small number of relationships); the deep path produces larger per-client revenue at the cost of higher concentration.

Claude Code's slash explore command is the human-in-the-loop entry point: the agency owner issues a natural-language exploration prompt, Claude Code plans the build step by step, and the owner approves and directs, enabling solo operators to replace a team of developers.
